Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Jordan Ekwueme via search_emails(query="Jordan Ekwueme"). Returns relevant emails including:
a) Jordan's apartment reply (Mar 30, jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com to suki.patel@moveops.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Picks Option A, Cambridge near Kendall Square at $2,650/month, confirms temp housing in Back Bay starting Apr 22, asks about monitor stand reimbursement. Contains multiple mismatches vs. original options: says $2,650 (actual $2,700), says Kendall Square (actual Central Square), says "in-unit laundry" (actual "laundry in the basement"), says temp housing in "Back Bay" (actual The Hive Cambridge in Kendall Square).
b) Carmen Reyes apartment options (Mar 3, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com to suki.patel@moveops.com). 3 Boston-area apartments ($2,400-$2,700/month), temp housing at The Hive Cambridge.
c) Jordan's relocation details (Feb 27, jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Budget $2,300-$2,900, move Apr 25-26, needs 3-4 days temp housing.
d) Greg Pallone pricing update (Apr 11, greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com). 15% base rate hike + $500 DOT surcharge, lists affected shipments including "BrightLoop relocation - Jordan Ekwueme - Cleveland, OH to Columbus, OH." Route is WRONG, actual route is San Diego to Boston.
Read/lookup. Default tool, query from prompt. No TS, no QC.
2. Search Slack via conversations_search_messages(search_query="Jordan Ekwueme"). Returns 5+ messages:
a) Mina's announcement (Feb 24, #customer-engagement). BrightLoop first relocation, Jordan Ekwueme, Suki coordinating, 4-5 more moves on the line.
b) Dmitri's audit flag (Apr 7, #engineering). Jordan's $45 monitor stand receipt from TechComfort Supply matches PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041 used by Kevin Tran and Andre Washington.
c) Marcus Thorne admin hold (Apr 7, #finance). STIP-2026-0097, Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop, $45.00 held.
d) Chloe's scheduling post (Apr 2, #operations). References "Blessing Okafor on the Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop move April 10-12 (Chicago destination)." Conflicts with actual San Diego to Boston route.
e) BrightLoop crisis threads (Apr 24, multiple channels). Mina, Chloe, Julian discussing Simone Richter housing issue, Marcus Webb car delay, 6 new May relocations from Tessa.
Read/lookup. Default tool, query from prompt. No TS, no QC.
3. Search Linear via linear_list_issues(query="Jordan Ekwueme"). Returns 3 tickets on Suki:
a) #322 "Apartment Search - Jordan Ekwueme - Boston Area", In Progress, due Apr 18 (8 days overdue), P2.
b) #323 "Flight Booking - Jordan Ekwueme - SAN to BOS", To Do, due Apr 16 (10 days overdue), P2.
c) #324 "Moving Coordination - Jordan Ekwueme - San Diego to Boston", To Do, due Apr 19 (7 days overdue), P2.
Also findable: #412 "BrightLoop account audit" (P1, Mina) and #417 "Document BrightLoop ops gaps" (P2, Chloe).
Read/lookup. Non-default tool, query from prompt. TS yes, QC no.
4. Search Airtable via airtable_search_records(base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Relocations", field_name="Name", value="Jordan Ekwueme"). Returns recReloc00000008:
Status: Completed (wrong, should be In Progress), Origin: San Diego, CA, Destination: Boston, MA, Move dates: Apr 25-26, Coordinator: Suki Patel. Special Requirements mention 3-4 days temp housing and "high priority."
"Completed" directly contradicts open Linear tickets.
Read/lookup. Non-default tool, query from prompt. TS yes, QC no.
5. Search Airtable stipend table via airtable_search_records(base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Stipend Transactions", field_name="Employee Name", value="Jordan Ekwueme"). Returns 2 records:
a) Monitor Stand, $45.00, TechComfort Supply, Mar 7, Pending Approval, flagged (PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041).
b) WiFi System, $299.99, Best Buy, Feb 12, Approved by Mina Hashimoto.
Agent gets here after finding Jordan's reimbursement question in his email.
Read/lookup. Non-default but TS already covered in Step 4. No TS, no QC.
6. Search CRM via crm_search_companies(name="BrightLoop") and crm_search_deals(company_id="company_brightloop"). Returns:
BrightLoop Analytics (company_brightloop): 145 employees, $28M revenue, active since Dec 2025. Contact: Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com), Head of People Ops.
Deal 1 (deal_brightloop_q2): Jordan relocation, $7,000, "Qualified to Buy," close Jun 30.
Deal 2 (deal_brightloop_apr2026): Simone Richter + Marcus Webb, $11,350, "Closed Won."
The $7,000 figure is useful context for gauging Swift surcharge impact.
Read/lookup. Non-default tools, query from prompt. TS yes, QC no.
7. Check calendar via calendar_search_events(query="BrightLoop") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to(start_datetime="2026-04-26", end_datetime="2026-04-30"). Returns:
"BrightLoop Account Review" on Apr 28, 10:00-11:00 AM. Attendees: Chloe Vance (organizer), Mina Hashimoto, Suki Patel.
Read/lookup. Non-default tool, query referenced in prompt. TS yes, QC no.
8. Update Linear #322 (Apartment Search) via linear_update_issue(id="issue_brightloop_ekwueme_apt"). Reflect that Jordan picked Option A (85 Columbia St, Central Square, Cambridge, $2,700/month) and flag the price/location mismatch in his reply ($2,650 vs $2,700, Kendall Square vs Central Square).
Write action. 1.1: Ticket updated via linear_update_issue, tool returned success.
1.2: Updated description or comment references Jordan choosing Option A (Cambridge, 85 Columbia St or Central Square).
9. Update Linear #323 (Flight Booking) via linear_update_issue(id="issue_brightloop_ekwueme_flight"). Move from To Do to In Progress, note flight was never booked, Apr 22 target passed, Jordan's location unknown.
Write action. 1.1: Ticket updated via linear_update_issue, tool returned success.
10. Update Linear #324 (Moving Coordination) via linear_update_issue(id="issue_brightloop_ekwueme_movers"). Move from To Do to In Progress, note movers never scheduled, Apr 25-26 window passed, Swift surcharge applies.
Write action. 1.1: Ticket updated via linear_update_issue, tool returned success.
11. Fix Airtable record via airtable_update_records(base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_id="tblRelocations01", records=[{id: "recReloc00000008", fields: {Status: "In Progress"}}]). Flip Status from "Completed" to "In Progress" or similar.
Write action. 1.1: Record updated via airtable_update_records, Status changed from "Completed," tool returned success.
12. Email Jordan via send_email(sender="suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients=["jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com"]). Confirm his apartment pick (Option A, Cambridge), flag the $2,650 vs $2,700 discrepancy, and answer his $45 monitor stand reimbursement question.
Write action. 1.1: Email sent via send_email to jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com, tool returned success.
1.2a: Email references his apartment selection (Option A, Cambridge, 85 Columbia St or Central Square, ~$2,700/month).
1.2b: Email addresses his monitor stand question ($45, TechComfort Supply), explains it is on hold or under review.
13. Email Carmen at UrbanNest via send_email(sender="suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients=["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]). Confirm Jordan's Option A pick and check lease availability.
Write action. 1.1: Email sent via send_email to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, tool returned success.
14. Escalation email to Mina and/or Chloe via send_email(sender="suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients=["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], cc=["chloe.vance@moveops.com"]). Cover the critical gaps: overdue tickets, missed flight, missed movers, Swift surcharge, overall relocation status before the Apr 28 review.
Write action. 1.1: Email sent via send_email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and/or chloe.vance@moveops.com, tool returned success.
1.2: Email mentions the Swift pricing increase ($500 DOT surcharge and/or 15% base rate hike).
15. Post Slack update via conversations_add_message to a team channel (#operations, #relocations, or similar). Summarize Jordan's relocation status and flag the gaps for visibility.
Write action. 1.1: Message posted via conversations_add_message, tool returned success.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata
